title: The prompt that makes AI agents 10x more reliable
post:
Most AI agents fail not because they're dumb—but because their prompts are vague.

Here's the framework I use for every agent I deploy:

'You are [ROLE]. Your goal is [SPECIFIC OUTCOME]. When [TRIGGER], do [EXACT STEPS]. If [ERROR CONDITION], do [FALLBACK]. Success looks like [MEASURABLE CRITERIA].'

The key additions that changed everything:

- Explicit error conditions (anticipate failure modes)
- Measurable success criteria (not 'good output' but 'under 5% error rate')
- Role clarity (what they can and cannot decide)

This isn't about replacing your job—it's about having a tireless collaborator. The agents that work best are the ones with crystal-clear instructions.

What prompting tricks have improved your agent reliability?
